zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth Spencer於1571年出生在英國瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓時，她的父親Nicholas Spencer25歲，母親Frances Mathon21歲。她於1589年與Sir James Coleman在英國素福爾可布倫特 艾萊結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1630年7月在英國素福爾可素沃爾德享年59歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可素沃爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Spencer于1571年出生在英国瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓时，她的父亲Nicholas Spencer25岁，母亲Frances Mathon21岁。她于1589年与Sir James Coleman在英国素福爾可布倫特 艾萊结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1630年7月在英国素福爾可素沃爾德享年59岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可素沃爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Spencer was born in 1571, in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, her father, Nicholas Spencer, was 25 and her mother, Frances Mathon, was 21. She married Sir James Coleman in 1589, in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 1 daughter. She died in July 1630, in Southwold, Suffolk, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Southwold, Suffolk, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Spencer(이)가 1571년 영국 워윅실르 워레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Spencer(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Frances Mathon(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1589년 영국 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에서 Sir James Coleman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1630년 7월 영국 수폴크 소우스울드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 소우스울드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Spencer родилась в 1571 г. в местности вормлиитон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Spencer было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Frances Mathon, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James Coleman в 1589 г. в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла июль 1630 г. в местности соутволд, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности соутволд, суффолк, Англия.
